query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Impudent', 'Lacking discretion, incautious, rash', 'The reviewer described the tone as impudent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impugn', 'Attack the truth or honesty of', 'The essay highlights the impugn of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impuissance', 'Lacking strength, feeble, weak', 'Scholars noted a striking impuissance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impulsion', 'The act of impelling,  driving onward, or pushing', 'Scholars noted a striking impulsion between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impunity', 'Exemption from punishment', 'The essay highlights the impunity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impure', 'Not pure, mixed with extraneous matter, contaminated', 'The reviewer described the tone as impure, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impute', 'Attribute to', 'Her impute became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inaccessible', 'Not able to be accessed, out of reach, inconvenient', 'Over time, the community learned to inaccessible its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inaccurate', 'Mistaken or incorrect, not accurate', 'Critics argued that the plan might inaccurate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inactive', 'Not active, temporarily or permanently', 'What seemed inactive at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inadequate', 'Not adequate, unequal to the purpose, insufficient', 'Critics argued that the plan might inadequate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inadmissible', 'Not admissible, cannot be admitted as evidence at a trial', 'What seemed inadmissible at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inadvertent', 'Not resulting from deliberate planning; unintentional', '“His inadvertent mistake cost the company thousands.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inadvisable', 'Unwise, not recommended, not prudent, not to be advised', 'Over time, the community learned to inadvisable its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inalienable', 'Incapable of being alienated, surrendered, or transferred', 'What seemed inalienable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inane', 'Silly or stupid', 'Her inane became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inanimate', 'Lacking the quality or ability of motion', 'Over time, the community learned to inanimate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inapprehensible', 'That cannot be apprehended, not apprehensible', 'The essay highlights the inapprehensible of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inapt', 'Not apt, unsuitable, inept', 'What seemed inapt at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inarticulate', 'Lacking the ability to express oneself, esp. through speech', 'Over time, the community learned to inarticulate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inaudible', 'Not able to be heard or not loud enough to be heard', 'The author hoped the new policy would inaudible tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inaugurate', 'To induct into office with a formal ceremony', 'Over time, the community learned to inaugurate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inauspicious', 'Not auspicious, ill-omened, unfortunate, unlucky', 'What seemed inauspicious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inborn', 'Naturally present at birth, innate', 'Her inborn became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inbr', 'Resulting from or involved in inbreeding', 'Her inbr became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incandescence', 'Intensely bright, brilliant', 'The essay highlights the incandescence of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incantation', 'The uttering of words purporting to have magical power', 'Over time, the community learned to incantation its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incapacitate', 'To make incapable (of doing something)', 'The author hoped the new policy would incapacitate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incapacity', 'The lack of a capacity, an inability', 'Scholars noted a striking incapacity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incarcerate', 'To imprison, confine', 'Over time, the community learned to incarcerate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incarnate', 'Embodied in flesh, given a bodily, especially a human, form', 'The author hoped the new policy would incarnate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incendiary', 'Designed to cause fires or stir up conflict', 'The author hoped the new policy would incendiary tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incense', 'To anger or infuriate', 'Over time, the community learned to incense its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incentive', 'A thing that motivates or encourages someone to do something', '“The company offered bonuses as an incentive for employees to work overtime.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inception', 'The creation or beginning of something, the establishment', 'Her inception became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inceptive', 'Beginning, of or relating to inception', 'The author hoped the new policy would inceptive tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incessant', 'Continuing without pause or interruption', 'The passage presents a incessant portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inchmeal', 'By inch, little by little', 'Her inchmeal became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inchoate', 'Just begun and not fully formed', 'What seemed inchoate at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inchoative', 'At the beginning, still in an unformed state', 'The reviewer described the tone as inchoative, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incidence', 'The rate or range of occurrence of something', 'Her incidence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incident', 'An event or occurrence', 'Scholars noted a striking incident between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incidental', 'Loosely associated, existing as a by-product, tangent', 'Her incidental became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incinerate', 'To destroy by burning', 'The author hoped the new policy would incinerate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incipient', 'A beginning, or first stage', 'Scholars noted a striking incipient between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incisal', 'Relating to the cutting edge of the incisor and cusped teeth', 'The reviewer described the tone as incisal, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incise', 'To cut into, cut marks, figures, etc', 'Critics argued that the plan might incise the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incisive', 'Intelligently analytical and clear-thinking', 'The reviewer described the tone as incisive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incisor', 'One of the front teeth of mammals, between the canines', 'The essay highlights the incisor of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Incite', 'To rouse, stir up or excite', 'The author hoped the new policy would incite tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
